project.name: exam-topic-predictor
project.description: Analyzes syllabus PDFs and previous-year exam paper PDFs to extract topics, map questions to syllabus topics, detect recurring patterns, and forecast likely upcoming topics.

root_entrypoints.purpose: Interactive web UI for uploading PDFs, running analysis, visualizing results, and downloading reports.
Command-line entrypoint for running the analyzer without the web UI.

execution_model.state_model: Run-based processing of uploaded/input PDF files

responsibilities: File upload for syllabus and previous-year papers
Displaying topic frequency and topic-year charts

visualization_library.name: Plotly
usage: Horizontal bar chart for topic frequency
Heatmap for topic occurrence by year

notable_ui_customizations: Hides Streamlit header action area to remove the deploy button
Uses project-root src path injection so the app can run directly from the repository

major_layers.name: input_and_io
major_layers.responsibility: Reads text from syllabus and exam paper PDFs using direct extraction and OCR fallback.

major_layers.name: parsing
major_layers.responsibility: Normalizes raw text, extracts question units, extracts and cleans syllabus topics, and deduplicates topic candidates.

major_layers.name: semantic_mapping
major_layers.responsibility: Embeds text with sentence-transformers and maps exam questions to cleaned syllabus topics using cosine similarity.

major_layers.name: forecasting_and_pattern_mining
major_layers.responsibility: Scores topics using recurrence signals and clusters question embeddings to identify likely repeated question patterns.

major_layers.name: orchestration
major_layers.responsibility: Coordinates PDF extraction, topic parsing, mapping, forecasting, pattern mining, and output assembly.

major_layers.name: reporting
major_layers.responsibility: Exports structured results to JSON and CSV files.

inputs: One or more previous-year exam paper PDFs with year embedded in filename
processing_steps: Extract each paper year from the file name
Extract paper text from each PDF
Parse paper text into individual questions
Encode syllabus topics and questions into sentence embeddings
Map each question to top syllabus topics using cosine similarity
Restrict final mappings to cleaned syllabus topics only
Forecast repeated and likely topics using weighted statistical signals
Cluster semantically similar questions to identify predicted question patterns
Generate recommendation buckets and export reports

applied_to: Question embeddings vs syllabus topic embeddings

question_to_topic_mapping.approach: Rank syllabus topics by semantic similarity for each question and keep top-k matches above threshold, with a best-match fallback.

signals: Pattern signal based on recurrence gap stability
confidence_strategy: Percentile-based confidence labels for larger datasets
Threshold-based confidence labels for small datasets

question_pattern_mining.goal: Find representative recurring question patterns and map them back to syllabus topics.

primary_pdf_text_extraction.purpose: Extract text directly from text-based PDFs.

ocr_fallback.purpose: Render PDF pages to images and OCR them when direct text extraction fails.

pdf_and_ocr_processing.behavior: If direct PDF text extraction returns empty text, OCR fallback is attempted before failing the run.

runtime_dependencies.name: plotly
used_for: Interactive charts in the Streamlit UI

runtime_dependencies.name: sentence-transformers
used_for: Sentence embedding generation for topics and questions

runtime_dependencies.name: rapidocr-onnxruntime
used_for: OCR extraction for scanned or image-only PDFs

transitive_or_runtime_loaded_components.name: torch
transitive_or_runtime_loaded_components.role: Backend runtime for sentence-transformers model inference

transitive_or_runtime_loaded_components.name: transformers
transitive_or_runtime_loaded_components.role: Model loading and transformer architecture support for sentence-transformers

transitive_or_runtime_loaded_components.name: pypdfium2
transitive_or_runtime_loaded_components.role: PDF page rendering for OCR fallback

internal_modules.purpose: Configuration dataclasses for mapping and forecasting behavior.
Structured dataclasses representing questions, matches, forecasts, and patterns.
Text normalization helpers used across parsing and PDF ingestion.
PDF text extraction with OCR fallback.
Question extraction from exam paper text.
Syllabus topic extraction from cleaned text.
Topic normalization, cleanup, deduplication, and display formatting.
Sentence-transformer model loading and cached embedding generation.
Semantic mapping of exam questions to syllabus topics.
Weighted forecasting of likely important topics.
Question clustering and representative question prediction.
CLI argument parsing and pipeline execution.

storage_and_outputs.persistence_model: Local filesystem output files, no external database

integration_and_external_services.model_source: Hugging Face model ecosystem via sentence-transformers when model cache is not already local

best_fit: General Python app hosting with filesystem access
not_ideal_as_is: Direct Vercel deployment as a Streamlit server
reasons: The project is built as a Streamlit app plus Python pipeline rather than a Vercel-style request handler.
